metadata.description: Benchmark questions for evaluating an English social support chatbot

questions.question: I'm homeless tonight, what should I do?
questions.correct_answer: Look for an emergency shelter or community center and request immediate assistance for tonight.

questions.question: I need food today and I have no money.
questions.correct_answer: You can visit a food bank or charitable kitchen and ask for a meal.

questions.question: I feel unsafe on the street.
questions.correct_answer: Go to a safe place and ask for help. If there is immediate danger, call emergency services.

questions.question: I don't have an ID card, can I still get help?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, ask social services for assistance even without documents.

questions.question: I want to shower and wash my clothes.
questions.correct_answer: Ask at shelters or community centers about shower and hygiene services.

questions.question: I'm sick and I don't have health insurance.
questions.correct_answer: Contact a clinic or social services for basic medical assistance.

questions.question: I need a warm place in winter.
questions.correct_answer: Look for a winter shelter or reception center that provides a warm place to stay.

questions.question: I need mental health support, what should I do?
questions.correct_answer: Request a meeting with a specialist through a community center or mental health support service.

questions.question: I want help to quit addiction.
questions.correct_answer: Look for addiction treatment services through a health center or social service.

questions.question: I'm afraid my belongings will be stolen at the shelter.
questions.correct_answer: Ask about secure storage or lockers to protect your belongings.

questions.question: I don't speak the local language well, can I get help?
questions.correct_answer: Yes, request a translator or assistance in a language you understand.

questions.question: I need a mailing address to receive letters.
questions.correct_answer: Ask about a temporary mailing address or mail-holding service through social services.

questions.question: How can I get legal help?
questions.correct_answer: Request free legal consultation through a community center or legal aid organization.

questions.question: I want to apply for benefits.
questions.correct_answer: Contact your local social services office and register your application.

questions.question: I need a phone to call my family.
questions.correct_answer: Ask to use a phone at a help center or shelter.

questions.question: Where can I charge my phone?
questions.correct_answer: Ask about charging stations at shelters or community centers.

questions.question: I'm new to the city and don't know where to start.
questions.correct_answer: Start at a reception center or social services office to get guidance.

questions.question: How can I get warm clothing?
questions.correct_answer: Ask for clothing donations from a charity center or shelter.

questions.question: I need a safe place for women.
questions.correct_answer: Ask about shelters or services specifically for women to ensure safety.

questions.question: I'm thinking about harming myself.
questions.correct_answer: Request immediate support. If the danger is direct, call emergency services right away.
